title: Medical Necessity – Imaging (CT/MRI)
text: Coverage for advanced imaging requires documented clinical indication, relevant prior conservative treatment when applicable, and alignment with evidence-based guidelines. For urgent red-flag symptoms, imaging may be approved without step therapy. Denials commonly occur due to missing indication or incomplete documentation.

title: Prior Authorization – Outpatient Procedures
text: Prior authorization decisions are based on benefit coverage, network status, diagnosis/procedure code pairing, and clinical criteria. Pended status may indicate missing documentation (e.g., provider notes, imaging reports) or pending medical director review. Provide clinical notes and rationale to support approval.

title: Claims Pend/Denial Guidance – Documentation Checklist
text: For PENDED or DENIED claims, validate member eligibility, provider network/authorization, correct coding, and atupporting documentation. If denial reason is clinical, submit records supporting medical necessity and confirm service dates and place-of-service are consistent with authorization.

title: Behavioral Health – Coverage Basics
text: Behavioral health services require eligibility confirmation and may require prior authorization depending on level of care. Ensure diagnosis aligns with covered services and include treatment plan details for continued stay requests.
